text.title: growth-seo-fundamentals Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-seo-fundamentals as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This is the single most common technical SEO disaster — frequently introduced during development and left deployed. Results in zero indexed pages. Always verify robots.txt before and after any site launch.

failure-mode.description: Keyword stuffing — repeating the target keyword unnaturally throughout the content
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Google penalizes keyword stuffing as a spam signal. Semantic search understands topical relevance without keyword repetition; natural language performs better.

failure-mode.description: Targeting high-volume keywords without assessing difficulty
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: New or low-authority sites cannot rank for competitive head terms. Starting with long-tail keywords (lower volume, lower difficulty) builds authority that eventually enables ranking for head terms.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: New content takes 3-6 months to rank; authority building takes 12+ months. Abandoning SEO due to no visible results at 30 days is the most common reason SEO investments fail.
